preview.summary: A Group A opener pairing Asia's most consistent qualifier against a Czech Republic side back at the finals after a 20-year absence. South Korea's technical, pacey attack meets a physical Czech team that thrives on set pieces and aerial duels, making this an intriguing clash of styles.
preview.homeForm: South Korea were the only Asian side to go unbeaten in qualifying, though recent friendlies were rocky, including a 4-0 loss to Ivory Coast and a 1-0 defeat to Austria.
preview.awayForm: Czech Republic booked their return to the World Cup after 20 years by winning playoff penalty shootouts against the Republic of Ireland and Denmark.
preview.homeKey: Son Heung-min, talisman chasing his country's World Cup scoring record.
preview.awayKey: Czech Republic's set-piece and aerial threat, their main route to goals.
storylines: Son Heung-min plays his fourth World Cup, one goal from history
Czech Republic return to the finals after two decades away
Korea's flair versus Czech physicality and dead-ball danger
preview.h2h: The two nations have rarely met competitively, with no prior World Cup meeting between them.
